title: Bisque Business: A Creamy Celebration
story: Chefs across America fire up their stoves on October 19 for National Seafood Bisque Day! This creamy, dreamy soup gets the spotlight it deserves. Restaurants feature special bisque menus with lobster, crab, and shrimp varieties. Home cooks share their family recipes online, while food bloggers post steaming photos that make everyone hungry. The rich, velvety soup originally came from France but has become an American favorite worth celebrating.
activity_question: What types of seafood are commonly used in bisque?
bonus_fact: The word 'bisque' comes from the French word meaning 'twice cooked.'

title: Too Much Info Day!
story: Your brain gets bombarded with thousands of messages, notifications, and facts every single day! Information Overload Awareness Day on October 19 reminds everyone to take a break from all the digital noise. People celebrate by turning off their phones for a few hours, reading actual books instead of scrolling, and focusing on just one thing at a time. It's the perfect excuse to ignore that pile of homework and just chill!
activity_question: What do people do on Information Overload Awareness Day to take a break from digital noise?
activity_choices: Turn off their phones for a few hours
Send more text messages than usual
Play video games for 10 hours straight
activity_answer: Turn off their phones for a few hours
follow_up_question: What would be the hardest digital device for you to give up for a day?
